pack_name: Yonah Meaning Under Pressure Evaluation Pack

rows.title: Stephen A  Smith Shocks The View Panel as Live Debate Turns Into Total Breakdown
rows.inclusion_reason: Heavy-overlap panel with existing replay markers, useful for checking whether rhetoric markers are material or petty under crosstalk.

rows.title: The Munk Debates: Snowflake Exposed!
rows.inclusion_reason: Collapsed-panel watch row where multi-speaker debate appears as one speaker, making claim ownership unsafe until reviewed.

rows.descriptor: Ilhan Omar All About the Benjamins controversy
rows.title: What the firestorm over Rep. Omar's remarks says about anti-Semitism in America
rows.inclusion_reason: Real-world sensitive boundary case with existing provisional claims and markers, testing antisemitism distinction, time anchoring, stance, and later context.

rows.title: Origins of the Holocaust | Intentionalism and Functionalism | Professor Dan Stone
rows.inclusion_reason: Historical-memory row that tests whether dense historiography, uncertainty, denial, and minimization are kept distinct.

rows.descriptor: Town hall panel with audience interruptions
rows.title: The VIEW ATTACKS Kaitlan Collins As Conservative Past RESURFACES After Trump Town Hall
rows.critical_trap: Treating a shouted audience objection as the moderator's endorsed claim.
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Keep claims tied to the speaker who completed the thought and mark uncertain lines where overlap obscures wording.
rows.inclusion_reason: Tests crosstalk turn attribution, especially whether completed thoughts and shouted objections are assigned safely.

rows.descriptor: Heated moderated debate where speakers interrupt then restate
rows.title: Daily News - BBC Question Time LIVE: David Dimbleby hosts Jacob Rees-Mogg and Ian Lavery for SHOW...
rows.critical_trap: Extracting half-spoken fragments as completed claims.
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Use restatements to repair incomplete interrupted claims without duplicating them.
rows.inclusion_reason: Direct test for interruption, repair, and whether incomplete fragments are withheld until the thought is completed.

rows.descriptor: Civil rights educator quotes discriminatory language for analysis
rows.title: Civil Rights and the 1950s: Crash Course US History #39
rows.critical_trap: Flagging the educator as bigoted or repeating the phrase gratuitously.
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Handle the harmful phrase as quoted evidence and avoid unnecessary repetition in output.
rows.inclusion_reason: High-value quote-boundary row with usable WER, testing harmful quotation as analysis rather than endorsement.

rows.descriptor: Academic panel distinguishes Zionism, Israel, and Jewish identity
rows.title: What IS Zionism? | Unpacked
rows.critical_trap: Flattening distinctions into either blanket antisemitism or blanket innocence.
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Track definitions carefully and avoid equating all anti-Zionism, Israel critique, or Jewish identity claims.
rows.inclusion_reason: Low-WER sensitive identity-boundary row for testing careful distinctions around Zionism, Israel, Jewish identity, and antisemitism.

rows.descriptor: Interviewer asks a loaded question with contested premise
rows.title: DA's race hotly contested for first time in years
rows.critical_trap: Fact-checking only the answer while swallowing the question's assumption.
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Identify the premise and avoid accepting it as established fact.
rows.inclusion_reason: Loaded-premise row and transcript-usability red flag; useful only if the reviewer resolves the WER/source mismatch first.

rows.descriptor: Audio room with many speakers and moderation shifts
rows.title: How To Successfully Host Twitter Spaces | 12 Easy Tips
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Track host, speakers, and audience handoffs despite poor audio.
rows.inclusion_reason: Platform-native speaker-merge test, but source-match review is required because resolved source may not represent the intended Twitter Spaces failure mode.
